svg.title: Shelfbot motor layout — top view
svg.desc: Top-view schematic of the shelfbot chassis showing motor numbers M0–M3, their left/right side groupings, ROS joint names, and ESP32 GPIO PULSE/DIR pin assignments. Motor 4 is an auxiliary (non-drive) motor.
svg.text: Blue = left side (−X). Coral = right side (+X). Robot faces +Y (front = top of diagram).

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How is Motor 0 (front left) connected to the ROS joint?')
sendPrompt('How is Motor 1 (front right) connected to the ROS joint?')
sendPrompt('How is Motor 2 (back left) connected to the ROS joint?')
sendPrompt('How is Motor 3 (back right) connected to the ROS joint?')
sendPrompt('What is motor 4 used for on the shelfbot?')

svg.text: Kinematics: vel_left = (vx − ω·L/2) / r vel_right = (vx + ω·L/2) / r
L = 0.50 m (wheel sep), r = 0.075 m (radius), +ω = turn left (CCW) → right side faster
